title: Patatas Aliñadas
subtitle: Spanish dressed boiled potatoes
excerpt: Warm boiled potatoes dressed while still hot with sherry vinegar, olive oil, raw onion, and flat parsley. A tapa from Andalusia that is more satisfying than its simplicity suggests.
sourceNotes: Patatas aliñadas are an Andalusian tapa, particularly associated with Seville and Cádiz. Documented in Penelope Casas, Tapas: The Little Dishes of Spain (1985). The dressing must go on while the potatoes are still warm. They absorb the vinegar and oil as they cool.

content.text: Patatas aliñadas work because the potatoes are dressed while still warm and then left to sit. As they cool, they absorb the sherry vinegar and olive oil rather than sitting in a pool of dressing on the surface. The result is potatoes that taste of themselves and of the dressing in equal measure.

Use waxy potatoes. They hold their shape and have the right texture to absorb the dressing without falling apart. The onion should be very finely chopped; raw onion is the point, but it should be present as flavour rather than crunch.

Put the unpeeled potatoes in a large saucepan of well-salted cold water. Bring to a boil and cook until just tender when pierced, 18 to 22 minutes depending on size. Drain and leave until just cool enough to handle. Peel if preferred (the skin can be left on), then slice into 1 cm rounds or chunks.

While the potatoes are still warm, transfer to a serving bowl. Whisk the olive oil, sherry vinegar, salt, and pepper and pour over the potatoes. Add the finely chopped onion and parsley. Toss gently, the potatoes will begin to absorb the dressing. Taste and adjust the vinegar or salt. Leave for at least 15 minutes before serving; serve at room temperature.

items.fix: Always dress while still warm; the residual heat opens the potato's surface
items.cause: Dressed when too cold; the potatoes couldn't absorb the dressing

items.fix: Soak the chopped onion in cold water for 15 minutes and drain before adding, this removes sharpness while keeping the flavour

content.text: Patatas aliñadas are the potato dish of Andalusia, served at room temperature, eaten slowly, always with a glass of fino sherry or cold beer. They appear on every outdoor table in summer and alongside fried fish as the cooling element. The name simply means 'seasoned potatoes', which undersells them entirely.
